$comment: Canonical cross-stack probe-mode manifest. The engine is the source of truth: crates/networker-tester/src/metrics.rs (Protocol enum + Protocol::all_modes()). This file is the machine-readable copy every other stack is guarded against. Drift guards: crates/networker-tester/src/modes_manifest_guard.rs (Rust, bidirectional enum coverage), dashboard/src/lib/modes-manifest.test.ts (TS: mode-family.ts + testbed-constants.ts), tests/Networker.ControlPlane.Tests/ModesManifestTests.cs (C#: /api/modes catalog). When adding a Protocol variant, update this file in the same PR — all three guards fail otherwise. See also docs/deploy-config.md (valid modes tables).

fields.id: canonical wire id — Protocol Display output / TestConfig mode string
fields.level: tester = a networker-tester Protocol variant; runner = expanded by the agent/orchestrator, never sent to the tester binary as a mode
fields.catalog: true = exposed in the GET /api/modes UI picker (Protocol::all_modes() + runner-level entries)
fields.family: dashboard chip family (mode-family.ts): net | http | thru | page | other
fields.name/description/detail/group: UI catalog text — present only when catalog is true; must match Protocol::all_modes() and PlatformEndpoints.AllModes byte-for-byte

modes.name: TCP
modes.detail: TCP 3-way handshake timing to measure raw connection latency

modes.name: DNS
modes.detail: DNS resolution timing for the target hostname

modes.name: TLS
modes.detail: TLS handshake via rustls — reports version, cipher, ALPN, cert chain

modes.name: TLS Resume
modes.detail: Two fresh TLS handshakes with a real HTTP request; the second should resume

modes.name: Native TLS
modes.detail: Uses SChannel (Win), SecureTransport (macOS), or OpenSSL (Linux)

modes.name: UDP
modes.detail: UDP echo probe — measures RTT, jitter, and packet loss

modes.name: HTTP/1.1
modes.detail: Full HTTP/1.1 request: DNS + TCP + TLS + request/response

modes.name: HTTP/2
modes.detail: HTTP/2 over TLS with ALPN h2 negotiation

modes.name: HTTP/3
modes.detail: HTTP/3 over QUIC (UDP) — 0-RTT capable

modes.name: Curl
modes.detail: Spawns curl binary, captures per-phase timing from --write-out

modes.name: SDK Probe
modes.detail: Probes a customer-embedded LagHound endpoint — splits total time into DNS, TCP, TLS, network transfer, and server processing via Server-Timing

modes.name: H1
modes.detail: Fetches page manifest + assets using 6 parallel HTTP/1.1 connections (browser-like)

modes.name: H2
modes.detail: Same assets multiplexed over a single TLS/HTTP2 connection

modes.name: H3
modes.detail: Same assets multiplexed over a single QUIC connection

modes.name: H1
modes.detail: Chrome headless with HTTP/2 disabled — forces HTTP/1.1

modes.name: H2
modes.detail: Chrome headless with QUIC disabled — forces HTTP/2

modes.name: H3
modes.detail: Chrome headless with QUIC forced via origin flag + SPKI cert pinning

modes.name: Download
modes.detail: Large payload download via HTTP — measures sustained throughput

modes.name: Upload
modes.detail: Large payload upload via HTTP POST — measures sustained throughput

modes.name: Download H1
modes.detail: Throughput download forced over HTTP/1.1

modes.name: Download H2
modes.detail: Throughput download over HTTP/2 multiplexed stream

modes.name: Download H3
modes.detail: Throughput download over QUIC/HTTP3

modes.name: Upload H1
modes.detail: Throughput upload forced over HTTP/1.1

modes.name: Upload H2
modes.detail: Throughput upload over HTTP/2

modes.name: Upload H3
modes.detail: Throughput upload over QUIC/HTTP3

modes.name: Web Download
modes.detail: Download via /download endpoint route

modes.name: Web Upload
modes.detail: Upload via /upload endpoint route

modes.name: UDP Download
modes.detail: Bulk download via UDP throughput server (port 9998)

modes.name: UDP Upload
modes.detail: Bulk upload via UDP throughput server (port 9998)

modes.name: API Workloads
modes.detail: Measured /api/* JSON workload suite: api-users, api-transform, api-aggregate, api-search, api-compress (benchmarks/configs/apibench.json). nginx is skipped — it serves no /api/* endpoints.

groups.label: Network
groups.detail: Low-level connection probes. Measures DNS resolution, TCP handshake, TLS negotiation, and UDP round-trip independently — isolates each layer of the network stack.

groups.label: HTTP
groups.detail: Full HTTP request timing across protocol versions. Each probe does DNS + TCP + TLS + HTTP request/response and reports TTFB, total duration, and negotiated protocol.

groups.label: Page Load (Native)
groups.detail: Loads a page with multiple assets using the Rust HTTP stack (no browser). Compares H1 (6 parallel connections), H2 (multiplexed), and H3 (QUIC). Fastest, no rendering overhead.

groups.label: Page Load (Browser)
groups.detail: Same page load test but using real Chrome headless. Includes rendering, JavaScript, and browser networking. Measures what users actually experience — DOM loaded, full load, bytes transferred.

groups.label: Throughput
groups.detail: Sustained transfer speed tests with configurable payload sizes. Measures download and upload bandwidth across different HTTP versions and transport protocols.

groups.label: API Workloads
groups.detail: Per-request server computation benchmarks against the spec-measured /api/* endpoints (API-SPEC.md §4): dataset sorting, SHA-256 hashing, percentile aggregation, regex search, and zlib compression. Request shapes are frozen in benchmarks/configs/apibench.json so every language measures identical work.

cli_aliases.$comment: CLI-only input aliases (cli.rs parsed_modes / Protocol::from_str) — never appear as wire ids in results or TestConfig.

cli_shorthands.$comment: CLI aggregate inputs that expand to multiple tester modes (cli.rs parsed_modes).
